design_philosophy.core_loop: Destroy enemy formations to accumulate power-up points, then strategically choose power-ups from periodic blocks to enhance ship capabilities (speed, fire rate). Manage power-up decay by continuously engaging enemies.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward decision-making: some enemy formations grant points while others subtract them, requiring pattern recognition. Unavailable (grayed-out) power-ups are lethal obstacles, forcing tactical choices about which upgrades to pursue.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command of an elite Delta Patrol battle cruiser, tasked with eliminating alien threats in the Delta Sector while customizing ship loadouts mid-mission.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game enforces a dynamic power-up system where upgrades are temporary and decay over time, rejecting permanent progression. This creates a cycle where players must continuously engage combat to maintain effectiveness rather than coast on accumulated upgrades. The dual-function blocks (beneficial or deadly based on availability) embed decision-making into environmental hazards.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players have agency in selecting which power-ups to acquire based on current loadout and threat assessment. The choice to engage or avoid specific enemy formations directly impacts difficulty, as some subtract points. High score persistence incentivizes replayability and mastery.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups (increased speed, faster fire rate) earned through destroying enemy formations. Upgrades decay over time, requiring continuous combat engagement. Power-up selection is player-directed via menu system.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Accumulated power-up points unlock access to more powerful upgrade tiers. Grayed-out (unavailable) power-ups become visible and selectable as point threshold rises.
replayability_hooks: High score table tracking encourages score chasing
Dual-formation enemy types (beneficial vs. point-draining) create replay variation in optimal routes
Music/sound effects toggle provides alternate audio experience

world_building_techniques: Mission premise: player is elite Delta Patrol officer commanding a battle cruiser
Setting establishes Delta Sector as region with hidden dangers (lost ships, alien bandits)
Context frames combat encounters as threat elimination within a police force framework

faction_indicators: Delta Patrol: elite police squadron (implied player affiliation)
Unknown destructive forces: ambient threats in Delta Sector
danger_signals: Enemy formations that subtract power-up points signal treacherous encounters
Grayed-out power-up blocks function as environmental hazards and decision traps
Continuous power-up decay creates escalating pressure to maintain combat engagement
environmental_storytelling: Delta Sector geography implied through scrolling environment and hazard density
Lost ships and debris referenced as contextual dangers
Power-up blocks and enemy formations populate space as manifestations of sector instability
